commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review is needed for the macOS app-host output contract and contributor-supplied real behavior proof; automation cannot prove the contributor's macOS setup for them.; Cleared: No security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff adds internal socket/output bounds and tests without new dependencies, workflows, secrets, or permissions. commands.review_summary:
Adds a 1 MiB pending response cap to `requestJsonlSocket`, truncates macOS exec-host stdout/stderr before JSONL encoding, and adds TS/Swift regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +28, Other +93. Total +127 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main buffers newline-free socket data until newline/timeout, and the PR body includes a real Unix-socket before/after proof, though I did not rerun it in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed for the macOS app-host output contract and contributor-supplied real behavior proof; automation cannot prove the contributor's macOS setup for them.

target.title: feat(doctor): warn when cron delivery targets inactive channel
target.body:
Related: #97496 (generic channel-plugin activation blockers — complementary, does not inspect cron jobs)

## What Problem This Solves

Resolves a problem where a persisted cron job that pins a concrete `delivery.channel`
(for example `slack`) keeps that target after an update or plugin change deactivates the
channel plugin, then **silently fails to deliver at its next scheduled run** with no
warning ahead of time. `openclaw doctor` already inspects the cron store (legacy
migration, `payload.model` overrides) but never checks whether each job's concrete
delivery target still maps to an active channel plugin, so the drift is invisible until
the run fails.

## Why This Change Was Made

Adds an observer-only doctor advisory: when the cron store is inspected, each job whose
resolved primary delivery plan names a **concrete** announce channel is checked against
the active channel plugins for the current config, and a `Cron` note lists any whose
plugin is not active. It is detection only — it never repairs jobs, reactivates channels,
or writes config. Pseudo/relative targets are intentionally skipped because they resolve
at run time and cannot be validated ahead: announce-to-`last`, `webhook`, `none`, and
jobs with no explicit delivery object. The concrete-vs-pseudo decision reuses the existing
`resolveCronDeliveryPlan`, and availability reuses `listReadOnlyChannelPluginsForConfig`
with the same options the doctor channel checks use (`includeSetupFallbackPlugins: true`),
so configured channels are recognized even when no gateway is running and are never
mistaken for unavailable ones. Channel resolution is lazy (only when a job pins a concrete
channel) and best-effort (a resolution failure emits no advisory), so the common doctor
path pays no extra cost and the check never breaks the cron flow. The shared
`formatProviderCounts` helper is generalized to `formatSortedCounts` now that two
advisories use it.

## User Impact

Operators running `openclaw doctor` now get an early, actionable warning when a scheduled
cron job points at a channel whose plugin is no longer active — for example after removing
a channel or changing plugins — instead of discovering the silent delivery failure only
after the next run is missed. The note names the affected job(s) and channel(s) and points
to `openclaw cron list` / `openclaw cron show <job-id>` to reactivate the plugin or update
the target. Jobs that deliver to `last`, via webhook, or with no channel are unaffected, and
when every concrete target resolves to an active channel doctor prints nothing new.

## Evidence

Observer-only; no config schema, CLI flag, or persisted data-model change.

Focused tests (10 new collector tests + 30 existing doctor-cron tests, non-breaking wiring):

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
    src/commands/doctor/cron/warnings.test.ts \
    src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts --reporter=dot
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  40 passed (40)
```

Typecheck (core prod + core test lanes): 0 errors.

```
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json ...            # 0 errors
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json ...  # 0 errors
```

Real production-path run — the real `listReadOnlyChannelPluginsForConfig` resolver, the
real `resolveCronDeliveryPlan`, and the real `note` emit, driven against an isolated
`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` with telegram configured and slack absent (redacted token / temp paths;
no mocks):

```
resolved available channel ids (real resolver): ["telegram"]

◇  Cron ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  Cron delivery targets unavailable channels at <state>/cron/jobs.sqlite.│
│  - 1 job announces to a channel whose plugin is not active; the next    │
│    scheduled run will fail to deliver                                    │
│  - Channels: slack=1                                                     │
│  - Examples: weekly-report -> slack                                     │
│  Reactivate the channel plugin or update the job's `delivery.channel`   │
│  after reviewing with openclaw cron list and openclaw cron show <job-id>.│
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

The same run leaves the configured `telegram` job, an announce-to-`last` job, and a webhook
job unflagged, confirming concrete-vs-pseudo classification on the real path.

commands.author: Pick-cat

target.title: fix(infra): bound jsonl-socket response buffer to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`requestJsonlSocket` accumulates incoming socket data into a string buffer until a newline is seen or the socket closes. A buggy or hostile peer that streams data without newlines can force the runtime to keep buffering until timeout or OOM.

This PR bounds that pending JSONL line buffer and aligns the macOS exec-host producer so valid command responses stay below the new requester cap.

## Changes

- `src/infra/jsonl-socket.ts`
  - Adds `JSONL_SOCKET_MAX_BUFFER_BYTES = 1 * 1024 * 1024`.
  - Returns `null` and destroys the socket once the pending line buffer exceeds the cap.
- `src/infra/jsonl-socket.test.ts`
  - Adds a regression test for an oversized newline-free socket response.
- `apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/ExecApprovalsSocket.swift`
  - Adds `ExecHostOutputLimiter` for macOS exec-host responses.
  - Truncates stdout/stderr before building `exec-res`, so macOS producer output stays below the shared JSONL requester cap.
- `apps/macos/Tests/OpenClawIPCTests/ExecApprovalsSocketAuthTests.swift`
  - Adds coverage for oversized output truncation, UTF-8 tail preservation, and worst-case escaped stdout+stderr staying below the JSONL cap.

## Real behavior proof

### Requester overflow proof

- **Behavior addressed**: A peer streaming a newline-free body larger than the cap must not hang or grow memory until timeout; the call must return `null` quickly.
- **Real environment tested**: Local Node v22 workspace, real `net.createServer` / `net.Socket` over a Unix domain socket in `/tmp`.
- **Exact steps**: `node --import tsx /tmp/proof-jsonl-socket.mts` starts a server that streams 64 KiB chunks every 5 ms with no newline, then calls `requestJsonlSocket` with a 2 s timeout.
- **Observed result after fix**: `RESULT: result=null elapsedMs=104`.
- **Negative control on current main**: `RESULT: result=null elapsedMs=2016`.

### macOS producer compatibility proof

- **Behavior addressed**: A valid macOS `exec-res` response should not exceed the new 1 MiB requester cap after stdout/stderr truncation.
- **Proof shape**: The macOS producer caps stdout and stderr at 80,000 bytes each before JSON encoding. A worst-case JSON escaping probe with both stdout and stderr filled with escaped NUL output produced a 959,988-byte JSON response, below the 1 MiB requester cap.
- **Observed result**:

```json
{"bytes":959988,"under1MiB":true}
```

- **Swift coverage added**: `ExecApprovalsSocketAuthTests` checks oversized output truncation, valid UTF-8 tail preservation, and escaped stdout+stderr response size below 1 MiB.
- **Local limitation**: Swift tests were not runnable in this Linux checkout because `swift` is not installed locally; the PR CI `macos-swift` lane covers SwiftFormat and Swift tests.

## Evidence

Terminal proof (real socket, streaming peer without newlines):

```
$ node --import tsx /tmp/proof-jsonl-socket.mts
RESULT: result=null elapsedMs=104
```

Negative control (temporary revert to `origin/main`):

```
$ git checkout origin/main -- src/infra/jsonl-socket.ts
$ node --import tsx /tmp/proof-jsonl-socket.mts
RESULT: result=null elapsedMs=2016
$ git checkout HEAD -- src/infra/jsonl-socket.ts
```

Worst-case macOS `exec-res` JSON size probe:

```
$ node --input-type=module <<'EOF'
const max = 80_000;
const marker = '... (truncated) ';
const nulTail = '\\^@'.repeat(max - Buffer.byteLength(marker));
const response = { type: 'exec-res', id: 'test', ok: true, payload: { exitCode: 0, timedOut: false, success: true, stdout: marker + nulTail, stderr: marker + nulTail, error: null }, error: null };
const bytes = Buffer.byteLength(JSON.stringify(response));
console.log(JSON.stringify({ bytes, under1MiB: bytes < 1024 * 1024 }));
EOF
{"bytes":959988,"under1MiB":true}
```

Focused Vitest after the latest update:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/jsonl-socket.test.ts --run

Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  6 passed (6)
```

Whitespace/check sanity after the latest update:

```
$ git diff --check
# exit 0
```

Earlier type checks on this PR:

```
$ pnpm tsgo:core      # exit 0
$ pnpm tsgo:core:test # exit 0
```
